headline: SLB OneSubsea and Subsea7 Sign Collaboration Agreement with PETRONAS Suriname
summary: HOUSTON, April 07, 2026--Global energy technology company SLB (NYSE: SLB) today announced the signing of a strategic collaboration agreement between PETRONAS Suriname E&P B.V. ("PETRONAS Suriname"), a subsidiary of PETRONAS and Subsea Integration Alliance, comprising SLB OneSubsea and Subsea7. This partnership aims to unlock resources in Suriname’s emerging frontier basin through innovative and cost-effective subsea solutions.

headline: Oil Stocks Are Hot. Here Are 9 Worth Getting To Know.
summary: Oil and gas stocks have been big winners amid the Iran war and the jump in oil prices, though there are laggards too.  Breaking the energy sector into its constituent oil and gas industry groups helps pinpoint the leaders.  IBD tracks a total of 241 oil and gas industry stocks sorted into 10 separate industry groups.

headline: Not all oil stocks will benefit from Middle East conflict, JPMorgan says
summary: Investing.com -- Not all oil services companies stand to benefit from the Middle East conflict, JPMorgan analysts say, as the Iran war disrupts the upstream spending picture in ways that are cutting differently across the sector.

headline: Energy Stocks: Winners And Losers At The Beginning Of Q2 2026
summary: Q1 2026 was an exceptional quarter for energy stocks thanks to war in the Middle East, but Q2 2026 is not guaranteed to step in its footsteps.

headline: SLB LTD (NYSE:SLB) Passes Key Quality Investing Screens
summary: Quality investing focuses on durable companies with strong finances. SLB exemplifies this with high returns on capital, robust cash flow, and controlled debt, despite energy sector cycles.

headline: Oil Surges Past $110 as Trump Threatens ‘Hell’ for Iran — Which Energy Stocks Could Catch the Rally?
summary: Crude oil prices have surged past $110 a barrel amid rising tensions in the Middle East. Over the weekend, President Donald Trump warned that etfs">Iran it could face attacks on bridges and power plants if the vital Strait of Hormuz is not reopened. As a result, Brent crude (CM:BZ) hovered just around $110, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) (CM:CL) neared $112. Here are three energy stocks — Schlumberger (SLB), Devon Energy (DVN), and Baker Hughes Company (BKR) — that analysts say have upside
